title: Riverside Freight Yard: Classification Track Plan
strategies: round-robin baseline: send car i to track i % K -- balanced track loads but every block is scattered across all tracks, reproducing the evaluator's weak reference plan
marginal-cost greedy: place each car in arrival order on the track whose incremental split/mix/overflow cost is smallest, clustering same-block cars without revisiting earlier placements
block-partition construction: group whole blocks onto tracks (bin-packing blocks by size under capacity, splitting only the oversized blocks) so mixing is confined to the fewest, smallest blocks
relocation local search / metaheuristics: start from greedy or a block partition and relocate/swap cars via an O(1) cost delta, with seeded perturb-and-re-descend restarts to escape local optima
